title: Etsy Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript Summary
text: Etsy reported consolidated GMS of $2.9 billion, down 2.1% year-over-year, with revenue growing 3% to $648 million. The company achieved an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.7%, exceeding guidance. Gifting GMS grew 4% year-over-year and significantly outperformed the broader marketplace, with management noting strong Mother's Day and Father's Day results. The company reduced searches dominated by single sellers by approximately 50% and cut nearly identical listing appearances by over 70%, benefiting both buyers and small merchants. Etsy Insider, a paid beta program launching in mid-September, targets occasional shoppers with benefits including free shipping and exclusive access at the cost of a latte per month. Active buyers reached approximately 91.5 million, growing 1% year-over-year. The company reactivated 6+ million lapsed buyers while adding 5+ million new buyers. Habitual buyer frequency declined 3%, though retention improved slightly. Q3 consolidated GMS is expected to decline in the low single-digit percentage range. Management cited macroeconomic volatility and event-driven impacts as ongoing headwinds. CFO Rachel Glaser announced plans to retire after seven years with the company.
